title: coreopsis nodosa
intro: Coreopsis nodosa, also known as tickseed, is a perennial wildflower native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of sesquiterpenes and saponins. The health benefits of Coreopsis nodosa include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a popular remedy for skin issues and digestive problems. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical applications like salves and creams.
